description: Tools and techniques of the shadow trade — surveillance devices, infiltration gadgets, poisons, forgery, and covert operations.
abilities: Blink Pack

A magical messenger pack with a hidden switch in the clasp. When activated, the switch turns the bag's mouth into a portal to a shadow plane where secret objects may be stored. Deactivated, the bag functions normally. Items stored in the pack do not count against normal carrying limits. The pack is paired with a magic amulet; pressing the amulet's button teleports the Blink Pack into your hands from anywhere in the omniverse.
Blueprint

A magical folio that reveals the floorplan of a building when pressed against an interior wall. Magical ink appears, tracing the layout of the entire floor — room shapes, hallways, doors, and windows. It works only on artificial structures, not natural caverns or caves. When paired with the Seeker, it can also display the real-time locations of the Seeker and any creatures it detects.
Bounty

Placing a bounty on the head of a lesser foe or ordinary person, offering a valuable item as reward for their capture or death. Bounty hunters compete for the prize. The outcome is uncertain — the deed may be done swiftly, take considerable time, or backfire entirely if the target learns of the bounty and places one on your head in return.
Bricolage

Improvising useful devices from everyday objects found in a room, assembled in moments. Possible creations include a tripwire rigged to an auditory alarm, a poison trap set into the floor, a makeshift weapon capable of modest damage, or a small periscope for peeking over obstacles and around corners.
Cloner

A magic amulet for eavesdropping on communication devices. When held next to any spell or object that transmits messages, it clones the signal and establishes a telepathic link, letting you hear all transmitted messages in your mind. Only one device can be cloned at a time; pairing with a new source breaks the previous link.
Cosmopolitan

Drawing on deep knowledge of cosmopolitan cultures, cities, and nations to pass convincingly as a local when challenged. By mimicking a gesture, a common saying, or some other cultural idiosyncrasy, ordinary people are easily fooled into accepting the disguise.
Crypto

A ring of opaque glass that glows when brought near glyphs — writing on paper, stone etchings, or any inscribed surface. When activated, it translates languages and decodes secret messages, scrolling the translation around its band as you read. Once the translation is complete, it deactivates.
Disguise

Assembling a convincing disguise from objects found in bedrooms, armories, offices, or similar rooms. The disguise includes at least one signature element — a uniform, a membership insignia, or a style of dress — and automatically fools ordinary people, though lesser foes and powerful enemies may grow suspicious.
Don't you know who I am?

Impressing ordinary people and lesser foes by pretending to be someone notable. A knowing wink to an ally kicks off a cascade of increasingly fantastical claims about your fabricated identity, each party member building on the last until a captive audience of nearby creatures crowds around in awe.
Dossier

A magic book of any size — from pocket folio to sprawling tome — that copies things. Pressing any written or drawn document against its pages produces a legible but slightly imperfect duplicate inside.
Feather Hook
A magical grappling hook that gently glides upward like a balloon until it finds and latches onto a ledge above. Any length of rope can be tied to it.
Listener

A small magic gem that records nearby sounds for up to an hour. Squeezing it softly plays back the recording aloud. Recording again erases the previous capture. When paired with the Cloner by inserting it into the amulet's socket, the Listener can record intercepted communications directly.
Mimic

A magical pen that guides your hand to replicate any writer's personal style. A magical stamp on the opposite end forges official seals. By bringing the Mimic close to a handwritten document, it learns the handwriting, signatures, and seals contained within, and can reproduce them on command to create a single convincing forgery.
Needful Hilt

A magical weapon hilt that instantly morphs into any common weapon you imagine while holding it — a sword, dagger, axe, or anything similar. In any form it deals modest damage. When you no longer need the weapon, it reverts to a simple hilt.
Needful Key

A magical key that senses the mechanism of any physical lock and morphs itself to fit it perfectly. In its default form, the key has no teeth. Twisting the handle reverts it. Each new lock requires a moment of attunement, but the key remembers every lock it has previously defeated and can match them again instantly.
Nightcrawler

Legendary skill at charming contacts and extracting sensitive intelligence. Upon infiltrating a target's office, encampment, or home, you uncover hidden information — the identity of key associates, upcoming travel plans, embarrassing secrets, financial entanglements, true feelings about you, or deepest allegiances. The information surfaces through discovered documents: receipts, diaries, letters, and the like.
Persona

Carefully preparing a convincing alternate identity, complete with wardrobe, credentials, and backstory. At its simplest, the persona is that of an ordinary person — a farmer, artisan, or traveling merchant — allowing you to blend effortlessly into crowds. With greater effort, you can forge the identity of an officer such as a judge or military commander, granting access to restricted areas and the authority to give mundane orders. At its most ambitious, you become an aristocrat — a socialite, ambassador, or governor — opening the doors to exclusive gatherings, audiences with rulers, and even pardons for minor offenses.
Poison

Combining common ingredients from any well-stocked general store into a single dose of poison, deliverable by weapon, drink, meal, or direct contact — each dose affecting one creature. Effects range in potency: a mild blue compound that induces deep sleep, a white toxin that causes agonizing pain and silence, a purple formula that paralyzes for an extended period, a lethal black poison that kills lesser foes and ordinary people outright while dealing devastating damage to powerful enemies, or a colorless, symptomless agent that kills after a delayed period — though the most powerful foes can resist it.
Seeker

A levitating orb that fits in the palm of your hand and responds to telepathic commands. Nearly invisible but emitting a low hum as it moves. At basic use, it scouts just beyond your line of sight or into an adjacent room and reports how many creatures occupy the space. With greater effort, it provides a mental map of the room's layout, major features, and the exact positions of all creatures within.
Shadow

A magic cape that renders everything but your shadow invisible. Upon activation, the cape shimmers briefly before you and everything you carry vanish. For a short time you are invisible, though your shadow still appears when you move and disappears when you stand still. Ordinary people and lesser foes will not notice you unless you make significant noise. Attacking while invisible breaks the effect.
Shadow Cutter

A wearable bracelet that stretches into a hoop large enough to crawl through. When pressed against a solid surface and activated, it carves a hole of equal circumference up to an arm's length deep, instantly teleporting the removed material to a random shadow plane. A low buzzing sound is audible to anyone nearby while it operates. When finished, it collapses back to its original size.
Silver Tongue

Talking your way out of trouble when caught doing something prohibited or mischievous. By reading the situation and appealing to the right justification — a claim of authority, genuine ignorance, or a well-placed bribe — suspicion melts away. Misjudging the approach, however, leaves you no better off than before.
Spyglasses

Enchanted eyeglasses that grant sight in natural darkness as though it were daylight. They can be permanently upgraded over time: magnification for studying small details or distant objects clearly, infrared vision revealing heat signatures even through walls, illusion detection that causes false images to flicker and distort, and a dial on the side for toggling freely between modes.
Strap
Concealing up to two weapons within your clothing so naturally that they escape notice unless someone actively searches you, provided you are wearing enough to reasonably hide them.
The Artifice

A small mask that projects a flawless illusion, transforming your appearance and voice. It passively records the likeness of every creature you encounter nearby and remembers them indefinitely. Upon activation, it can project the appearance and voice of any creature in its memory, fooling everyone except that creature's closest friends and family.
Tracker

A pair of linked tracking devices: a magical tag the size of a tiny bead, sticky enough to adhere to most surfaces, and a magic compass whose arrow points toward the tag. The arrow turns from black to gold when the tag is nearby and begins to vibrate softly.
